title: Port marine operations officer
change: Occupational standard, assessment plan and funding band revised but funding band remained the same
overviewOfRole: Assists in ensuring the safe transit and handling of vessels into and out of the port.

knowledges.detail: Health and safety regulations, relevance to the occupation and responsibilities.
Methods of safe working including risk assessments, method statements, safe systems of work and control measure application.
Methods for managing identified or unforeseen risks.
Maritime sector legislation, regulations, statutory powers and local port marine codes of practice.
Port security legislation and the security levels that are associated with the port industry.
Principles and considerations for responding to incidents and emergencies.
Port marine standard operating procedures (SOP). What they are and why they are important.

Service level agreements (SLA’s), key performance indicators (KPI’s) and business operation consideration principles, including efficiency, customer satisfaction, competitiveness and ethical practices.
Principles of sustainability and environment safeguarding.
Environmental and sustainability regulations and legislation. Impact of the port marine sector on the environment. Special sites of scientific interest (SSSI) and marine conservation zones.
Principles and techniques of resource management including the impact of cost, quality, safety, security, the natural environment, and work scheduling.
Quality assurance procedures and monitoring processes.
Principles and techniques of port control operations for example port services, vessel traffic systems and shore-based port marine services.
Types of port marine infrastructure, for example, locks, dry docks, river berths, ro-ro bridges. Principles and techniques of monitoring and recording condition and maintenance requirements.
Principles and practice of vessel movements and berthing including pilotage.
Principles of coordinating, planning and scheduling vessel movements and berthing.
Principles and practices of shore side support including third party support services.
Types of marine craft and vessels and the operating limits of port authorities.
Principles and techniques of hydrographic surveying and conservancy and interpreting the data and information.
Principles and techniques of determining and reporting hydrological and meteorological conditions including tidal conditions and weather patterns.
Principles and techniques of adapting port marine operations in response to changing circumstances for example changing weather conditions.

Principles and techniques of monitoring and operating vessel and shore-based navigation systems, equipment and aids to navigation (AtoN) including automatic identification system (AIS), tide gauges, navigation lights.
Principles and techniques for using organisational and sector information technology and digital systems, complying with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and cyber security requirements.
Principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion in the workplace. Unconscious bias.
Personal wellbeing and mental health awareness.
Principles and methods of communicating using radio (VHF, UHF)  and signalling.
Collaborative working techniques with internal and external stakeholders.
Continuing professional development and recording methods.

behaviours.detail: Prioritise health and safety to support a safe working culture.
Act professionally, considering operational, ethical, safety and sustainability.
Respond and adapt to changing work demands and situations.
Contributes to equity, diversity, and inclusivity workplace culture.
Collaborate within teams, across disciplines and with internal and external stakeholders.
Committed to maintaining and enhancing competence of self through Continued Professional Development (CPD).

skills.detail: Apply safe systems of work and comply with health and safety regulations and procedures.
Promote safe working practices.
Develop and implement hazard and risk management systems, including risk assessments, method statements, safe systems of work and control measures.
Comply with maritime sector legislation, regulations, statutory powers and local port marine codes of practice.
Comply with port security measures.
Apply port marine standard operating procedures (SOPs).
Apply principles and practices of sustainability and environment safeguarding.
Identify, plan, prioritise, organise and use resources.
Apply quality assurance procedures and monitoring processes.
Coordinate and monitor port control operations.
Monitor and record the condition of port marine infrastructure.
Monitor, coordinate and record vessel movements.
Plan, schedule and coordinate berthing movements.
Coordinate shore side support to vessels.
Interpret hydrographic survey results or data.
Apply principles and techniques to determine and report hydrological and meteorological conditions, for example weather patterns or tidal conditions.
Adapt port marine operations to changing circumstances, for example, changing weather conditions.
Monitor and operate navigational systems, equipment and aids to navigation (AtoN).
Use organisational and sector information technology and digital systems. Comply with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and cyber security.
Apply and promote policies and practices to support equity, diversity and inclusion.
Communicate with others using a radio or signalling.
Work collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders, as part of a team.
Plan and undertake Continued Professional Development (CPD) to meet personal and organisational development. Evaluate CPD outcomes against any plans made.

Ofqual
Earlsdon Park, 53-55 Butts Road, Coventry, CV1 3BH

involvedEmployers: Associated British Ports, DP World, International Association of Maritime Institutions (IAMI), Marine Society & Sea Cadets, Maritime Skills Alliance, Peel Ports, Port of Blyth, Port of Dover, Port Skills and Safety LTD, Svitzer, Tyne coast college
englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Manage and lead the operation of a transport facility

duties.dutyDetail: Perform and monitor safe working practices within the working environment.
Comply with industry standards, relevant legislation, environmental regulations and standard organisational operating procedures.
Comply with port security; International ship and port facility (ISPS) code.
Promote sustainable practices and reduce environmental impact in port marine operations.
Plan and undertake port marine operations activities.
Monitor and organise vessel traffic movement inside and outside of the port.
Control and coordinate the movements of port craft to facilitate the safe movement inside and outside of the port.
Monitor and review external information and data which would impact port marine operations.
Work as part of a team supporting colleagues to meet customer expectations, quality requirements and deadlines.
Communicate and liaise with own team, the vessel, along with internal and external stakeholder such as clients or specialist contractors.
